qwen.status: UNAVAILABLE — endpoint up but embeddings HTTP 400: {"error":"Failed to load model \"text-embedding-qwen3-embedding-8b\". Error: Operation canceled."}
qwen.needed: the shipped Qwen H6 result uses Qwen3-8B embeddings + the Correct-&-Smooth content model in src/gnn_layer/discriminant.py; this lightweight pass cannot adjudicate the encoder cleanly without it

interpretation: ROBUSTNESS CAVEAT, NOT an H6 refutation. The shipped H6 (discriminant.py) finds probe ≫ content-similarity (Δκ ~ +0.17 human / +0.21 LLM) on QWEN3-8B embeddings with a Correct-&-Smooth content model. Here, on the WEAKER all-MiniLM-L6-v2 encoder and a kNN-majority content instrument, the sign FLIPS: content-similarity matches/edges out the linear probe (Δκ -0.05 5-class CI incl. 0; -0.09 6-class CI EXCLUDES 0). Two honest, non-exclusive reasons: (a) MiniLM-384d carries less linearly-separable VAAMR signal than Qwen-4096d, so a nonparametric kNN extracts more local structure than a linear probe — a CLASSIFIER-CAPACITY effect, not proof of topicality; (b) the 6-class 'No code' class is strongly content-clustered (kNN homophily rises 0.30->0.36 and content-sim's edge grows with 'No code' added), so Q27/Q28: 'No code' inflates the CONTENT model, not the probe. Bottom line: H6's probe≫content contrast is NOT shown to be encoder-robust by this proxy; a faithful Q8 test must re-run the SAME discriminant.py probe-vs-C&S instrument on MiniLM AND Qwen. The Qwen embedding model was listed but failed to load at run time.
note: Q8: H6 robustness to encoder choice (MiniLM, weaker than Qwen). Q27/Q28: 'No code' contribution = compare 5-class vs 6-class Δκ. Probe = logistic regression; content-sim = kNN-majority over cosine neighbours (a hard proxy for discriminant.py's Correct-&-Smooth); both on identical participant-grouped folds + embeddings. Chance κ = 0.
